paper_title: Analysis of Posttranslational Modification Sites in Diatom Tubulins

methods.description: This method involves searching for known post-translational modification sites in predicted amino acid sequences from five α-tubulin groups. The results are intended for use in theoretical and experimental reconstructions of cytoskeleton regulation during various stages of the diatom cell cycle.

steps.description: We performed a search for known post-translational modification sites in predicted amino acid sequences from five α-tubulin groups.
steps.searchable_summary: search for known post-translational modification sites in amino acid sequences

steps.description: It was shown that lysine in homolog positions to H. sapiens α-tubulin methylation and acetylation site is only retained in group α1.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze conservation of lysine in homologous positions to human α-tubulin

steps.description: The polyglutamylation sites of α-tubulin E443 and E445 are retained in 35 and 46 cases of 83, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze retention of polyglutamylation sites E443 and E445
outputs: retention statistics for E443 and E445

steps.description: The polyglycylation sites E446 and E448 in the sequences of diatom α-tubulins are almost not preserved.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze preservation of polyglycylation sites E446 and E448
outputs: preservation status of E446 and E448

steps.description: Sites that are homologous to H. sapiens phosphorylation and ubiquitinylation tyrosine Y432 and lysine K304, respectively, are also conserved for all groups.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze conservation of phosphorylation and ubiquitinylation sites homologous to human
outputs: conservation status of Y432 and K304

steps.description: Phosphorylation is the only type of modification described for γ-tubulin (S80, S131, T288 and S361).
